Super League Qualifying Play-Offs (for bottom 4 as well as top 4 of Championship |playoffs_link = |conf1 = |conf1_link = |conf1_champ = |conf1-runner-up = |conf2 = |conf2_link = |conf2_champ = |conf2-runner-up = |playoffs_MVP = |playoffs_MVP_link = |finals = |finals_link = |finals_champ = |finals-runner-up = |finals_MVP = |finals_MVP_link = |prevseason_link = Super League XXI |prevseason_year = 2016 |nextseason_link = Super League XXIII |nextseason_year = 2018 }} The '''Betfred Super League XXII''',{{cite web|title=First Utility powers title sponsorship of Super League|url=http://superleague.co.uk/article/29353/first-utility-powers-title-sponsorship|publisher=Super League|access-date=23 January 2014}} was the year 2017 [[Super League]] season and 123rd season of [[Rugby Football League Championship|rugby league in Britain]]. Super League XXII featured twelve teams, the third year in which this number has taken part. This was also the third year since promotion and relegation was reintroduced into the competition, seeing [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] promoted and [[Hull Kingston Rovers|Hull KR]] relegated from last season. ==Teams== Eleven teams in Super League are from the North of England. Six teams hail from the historic county of [[Lancashire]], west of the [[Pennines]]: [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]], [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]], [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]], [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]], and [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]]. Five teams hail from the historic county of [[Yorkshire]], east of the [[Pennines]]: [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]], [[Wakefield Trinity]], [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]], [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]], and [[Hull F.C.]] [[Catalans Dragons]], located in [[Perpignan]], France, are the only team outside the North of England. St Helens, Wigan Warriors, Warrington Wolves, and Leeds Rhinos are the only teams to have played in every season of Super League since 1996. Leigh were promoted from the [[Kingstone Press Championship]] after finishing in 2nd place in The Qualifiers for 2016. Leigh became the first club promoted to the Super League under the Super 8s system, and the first club promoted to Super League since Widnes received a license for [[Super League XVII]]. Leigh last competed in the top flight in [[Super League X]]. [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] were relegated to the Championship after losing the 2016 Million Pound Game to Salford. {{location map+ |Northern England|float=right |width=450 |caption=Locations of Super [[Iafeta Paleaaesina]] - {{leagueicon|Hull|16}} [[Hull F.C.|Hull]] ==Media== ===Television=== 2017 is the first of a five-year contract with Sky Sports to televise 100 matches per season.{{cite web |url= http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/9141015/rfl-chief-nigel-wood-delighted-with-new-sky-sports-rugby-league-deal|publisher= Sky Sports|title= Super League deal |author=Sky Sports |date=31 January 2014 |access-date= 9 October 2016}} [[Sky Sports]] coverage in the UK will see two live matches broadcast each week, usually at 8:00 pm on Thursday and Friday nights. {{cite web |url= http://www.skysports.com/story/0,19528,12944,00.html |publisher= Sky Sports |title= Rugby League live on Sky |author=Sky Sports |date=18 February 2012 |access-date=19 February 2012}} Regular commentators will be [[Eddie Hemmings (rugby league)|Eddie Hemmings]] with summarisers including [[Phil Clarke]], [[Brian Carney (rugby)|Brian Carney]], [[Barrie McDermott]] and [[Terry O'Connor (rugby league)|Terry O'Connor]]. Sky will broadcast highlights on Sunday nights on ''Super League - Full Time'' at 10 p.m. [[BBC Sport]] will broadcast a highlights programme called the ''[[Super League Show]]'', presented by Tanya Arnold. The BBC show two weekly broadcasts of the programme, the first to the [[BBC North West]], [[BBC Yorkshire|Yorkshire]], [[BBC North East and Cumbria|North East and Cumbria]], and [[BBC Yorkshire and Lincolnshire|East Yorkshire and Lincolnshire]] regions on Monday evenings at 11:35 p.m. on [[BBC One]],{{cite web |url= https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/16877011 |publisher= BBC Sport |title= BBC's Super League Show returns |author=BBC Sport |date=3 February 2012 |access-date=19 February 2012}} while a repeat showing is shown nationally on [[BBC Two]] on Tuesday afternoons at 1.30 p.m. The ''Super League Show'' is also available for one week after broadcast for streaming or download via the [[BBC iPlayer]] in the UK only.{{cite web |url= https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/b0071mmc |publisher= BBC |title= BBC One - Super League Show |author=BBC |access-date=19 February 2012}} End of season play-offs are shown on BBC Two across the whole country in a weekly highlights package on Sunday afternoons.{{cite web|title=BBC Two - Rugby League: Super League Play-Offs - Highlights|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/programmes/b00dqk4x|publisher=BBC|access-date=14 September 2013}} Internationally, Super League is shown live or delayed on [[ShowSports|Showtime Sports]] (Middle East), [[Sky Sport (New Zealand)|Sky Sport]] (New Zealand), [[TV 2 Sport (Norway)|TV 2 Sport]] (Norway), [[Fox Soccer Plus]] (United States), [[Fox Sports (Australia)|Fox Sports]] (Australia) and [[Sportsnet World]] (Canada). ===Radio=== BBC Coverage: * [[BBC Radio 5 Live Sports Extra]] (National DAB Digital Radio) will carry two Super League commentaries each week on Thursday and Friday nights (both kick off 8pm); this will be through the [[BBC Radio 5 Live|5 Live]] [[Rugby league]] programme which is presented by [[Dave Woods (commentator)|Dave Woods]] with a guest summariser (usually a [[Super League]] player or coach) and also includes interviews and debate.. * [[BBC Radio Humberside]] will have full match commentary of all Hull F.C. matches. * [[BBC Radio Leeds]] carry commentaries featuring Leeds, Castleford, Wakefield and Huddersfield. * [[BBC Radio Manchester]] will carry commentary of Leigh, Wigan and Salford whilst sharing commentary of Warrington with [[BBC Radio Merseyside]]. * [[BBC Radio Merseyside]] will have commentary on St Helens and Widnes matches whilst sharing commentary of Warrington with [[BBC Radio Manchester]]. Commercial Radio Coverage: * 102.4 [[Wish FM]] will carry commentaries of Wigan & St Helens matches. * 107.2 [[Wire FM]] will carry commentaries on Warrington Home and Away. * Radio Yorkshire will launch in March carrying Super League commentaries. * Radio Warrington (Online Station) all Warrington home games and some away games. * Grand Sud FM covers every Catalans Dragons Home Match (in French). * Radio France Bleu Roussillon covers every Catalans Dragons Away Match (in French). All Super League commentaries on any station are available via the particular stations on-line streaming. ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[http://www.superleague.co.uk/ Official Site] *[https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league BBC Rugby League] {{2017 in rugby league}} {{Super League Europe}} [[Category:2017 Super League season| ]]
